name: Red Wine Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 5 cups Tart apples, cored and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Place apples, 3/4 cup sugar, red wine, and lemon juice in a 3-quart saucepan. Cover and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally until apples are just tender, 7 to 8 minutes.
2. Mix 1/4 cup flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, and 1/8 teaspoon salt. Stir into the apple mixture. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly. Boil and stir 1 minute. Remove from heat. Stir in butter. Cool to room temperature.
3. Measure 2 cups flour, cheese, and 1 teaspoon salt into a bowl. Cut in shortening or lard until combined. Sprinkle with water, 1 tablespoon at a time, mixing until all flour is moistened and pastry almost cleans the sides of the bowl (up to 2 teaspoons water can be added if necessary).
4. Gather pastry into a ball. Divide in half and shape into two flattened rounds on lightly floured cloth-covered board. Roll one round until it is about 2 inches larger than an inverted 8-inch pie plate with a floured stockinet-covered rolling pin. Fold the pastry into quarters and place it in the pie plate.
5. Unfold the pastry and ease it into the pie plate, trimming any overhanging edge 1/2 inch from the rim of the pie plate.
6. Roll the second round of pastry. Fold into quarters, cut slits so steam can escape, and place over the filling. Unfold and trim the overhanging edge 1 inch from the rim of the pie plate. Fold and roll the top edge under the lower edge, pressing on the rim to seal; flute. Cover the edge with a 2- to 3-inch strip of aluminum foil to prevent excess browning.
7. Bake at 425°F for 25 to 30 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for an additional 15 minutes.
NOTE 1: If using self-rising flour, omit the 1 teaspoon salt. Pie crusts made with self-rising flour differ in flavor and texture from those made with plain flour.
NOTE 2: If desired, a package (11 ounces) of pie crust mix or sticks can be substituted for the pastry.
